questions.question: Which country has passed a legislation to study and promote legacies of Mahatma Gandhi and Martin Luther King?
questions.explanation: A Legislation has been passed by the USA’s House of Representatives that seeks to create an initiative to study and promote the legacies of Mahatma Gandhi and Martin Luther King. This paves way for an annual educational forum for scholars and researchers of both countries that would revolve around the preaching and ideologies of Gandhi – Martin Luther King.

questions.question: Hampi Chariot is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, located in which state?
questions.explanation: The famous stone chariot in front of the Vijaya Vittala Temple in the UNESCO World Heritage site of Hampi, has been covered by a protective ring by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).This chariot is among three famous stone chariots in India while the other two are in Konark, Odisha, and Mahabalipuram, Tamil Nadu. Hampi chariot, was built during the dynasty of Vijayanagara rulers who ruled from 14th to 17th century CE.

questions.question: The Central Government has decided to set up which organisation to boost AYUSH exports?
questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Commerce & Industry along with Union Ministry of AYUSH has decided to work together for establishing AYUSH Export Promotion Council. The establishment of this organisation would boost the AYUSH export to various international destinations. The two ministries also decided to review the entire AYUSH sector for achieving price and quality competitiveness.

questions.question: Which Indian bank has launched a new mobile banking application for customers of all banks, named ‘iMobile Pay’?
questions.explanation: ICICI Bank has recently launched a new mobile banking application named ‘iMobile Pay’, which can offer payments and banking services to customers of any bank.Through the app, customers can pay to any UPI (Unified Payments Interface) ID or merchants, pay bills and do online recharges among others. It also enables users to access instant banking services like loans, credit cards, gift cards, travel cards and more.

questions.question: Where is the Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Convention to be held this year?
questions.explanation: This year’s Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Convention will be held in a virtual mode, on account of COVID 19 pandemic movement restrictions. The virtual convention will be inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Pravasi Bharatiya Divas is observed on 9th January of every year that commemorates the return of Mahatma Gandhi from South Africa.

questions.question: The Armed Forces Flag Day is being celebrated in India since which year?
questions.explanation: The Armed Forces Flag Day is a day dedicated towards collection of funds from people of India for the welfare of the Indian Armed Forces personnel.India has been observing December 7 annually as the Armed Forces Flag Day since 1949 to honour the soldiers. The Flag day aims for rehabilitation battle casualties, welfare of serving personnel and their families as well as ex-servicemen and their families.

questions.question: Who is the first Indian Army Chief to visit the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Saudi Arabia?
questions.explanation: India’s Chief of Army Staff (COAS) Manoj Mukund Naravane has undertaken a “historic” six-day visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Saudi Arabia.This is first such visit by the Indian Army Chief to the two Gulf countries. This visit aims to strengthen the strategic and defence cooperation between the two countries. Saudi Arabia is India’s fourth-largest trading partner.

questions.question: Which country rolled out the first vaccine in the world?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom became the first country in the world to start using the COVID-19 vaccine. The vaccine developed by Pfizer and BioNTech was approved by the regulators.Several hubs across the country are vaccinating the people. The vaccine is given as two injections with 21 days apart. The complete immunity is said to be reached seven days after the second dose.

questions.question: What is the theme of the International Anti-Corruption Day 2020?
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly designated 9 December as International Anti-Corruption Day, every year. This year, the theme is ‘Recover with Integrity’.The day aims to raise awareness of corruption and of the role of the UN Convention in fighting and preventing it. In the year 2003, the UNGA adopted the United Nations Convention against Corruption.

questions.question: Which Indian teacher has been awarded the prestigious ‘Global Teacher Prize 2020’?
questions.explanation: Ranjitsinh Disale, who is a primary school teacher from the state of Maharashtra, has been awarded the most prestigious “Global Teacher Prize 2020”.He has been conferred with the USD 1 million Global Teacher Prize, for his contribution in the field of teaching and for injecting new ideas for girl education. This award was constituted by Varkey Foundation in partnership with UNESCO.
